name: Mithril
description: Lightweight JavaScript framework with built-in routing and XHR utilities for building fast SPAs

description.value: A modern client-side JavaScript framework for building SPAs with built-in routing and XHR utilities

initial-release.comment: First public release was in March 2014

active-maintenance.comment: v2.3.8 released November 2025, repository actively updated

reactivity-model.comment: Uses virtual DOM with efficient diffing and auto-redraw system when using m.mount()

component-model.comment: Components are objects with a view function that returns virtual DOM elements

state-management-approach.comment: Uses auto-redraw system with m.mount() for reactive state management; no external library required

opinionatedness.comment: Minimal, pragmatic API - doesn't prescribe specific patterns

backward-compatibility.comment: Generally maintains API stability across major versions

typescript-support.comment: Type definitions available via @types/mithril from DefinitelyTyped

template-syntax.comment: Uses hyperscript m() function; JSX supported via mithril-tsx-component or Babel plugin

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; uses standard CSS or any CSS approach

build-required.comment: Can be used directly from CDN without build step

reactive-primitives.comment: Uses auto-redraw system rather than reactive primitives like signals

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed values; uses plain JavaScript

two-way-binding.comment: Uses event handlers and oninput for form binding

conditional-rendering.comment: Uses JavaScript ternary operators and conditionals in hyperscript

list-rendering.comment: Uses JavaScript array methods like map() in hyperscript

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in context system; uses module-level state or closure patterns

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: DOM elements accessible via oncreate and onupdate lifecycle hooks

route-guards.comment: Supports onmatch hook for route guards

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Components can be loaded asynchronously via onmatch

scroll-restoration.comment: No built-in scroll restoration; requires manual implementation

built-in-state.comment: Uses closure variables and auto-redraw with m.mount()

built-in-global-state.comment: No official global state solution; uses module-level variables or external libraries

global-state-library.comment: No official recommendation; community uses various patterns

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No dedicated devtools; Meiosis Tracer available for Meiosis pattern

time-travel-debugging.comment: Available via Meiosis Tracer for apps using Meiosis pattern

official-cli.comment: No official CLI; uses standard npm/bundler workflows

hot-module-replacement.comment: Supported via standard bundler HMR (Vite, webpack, etc.)

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser devtools extension; Meiosis Tracer available for specific patterns

vs-code-extension.comment: Mithril Emmet extension for hyperscript/Emmet support

error-messages.comment: Basic error messages with component context

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with tutorials, API reference, and framework comparison

playground.comment: No official playground; can use flems.io or CodePen

bundle-size-full.comment: Same as core - routing and XHR are built-in

lazy-components.comment: Components can be loaded dynamically via async import

partial-hydration.comment: Hydration is a known challenge; community solutions exist but not built-in

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Mithril performs well in benchmarks but exact current geometric mean not verified

component-libraries.comment: Limited ecosystem; some UI libraries like Polythene available

discord-community-size.comment: Primary community on Zulip, not Discord

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Questions exist but community noted as smaller compared to major frameworks

job-market-demand.comment: Niche framework with very few job postings

official-testing-library.comment: Recommends ospec and mithril-query but neither is first-party

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with standard E2E tools like Playwright and Cypress

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; standard PWA techniques work

desktop-framework.comment: Can be used with Electron or Tauri but no official integration

learning-curve.comment: Simple API, minimal concepts, leverages plain JavaScript knowledge

concepts-to-learn.comment: m() hyperscript, components, m.mount/m.route, m.request - very minimal API

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes - can include from CDN and start immediately

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Can mount to specific DOM elements, works alongside other code
